meta.title: MiniMax Music 2.6 API | APIDot
meta.description: Use MiniMax Music 2.6 API on APIDot for AI song generation with lyrics, Lyrics Optimize, instrumental mode, audio output control, and fixed $0.10 pricing.

hero.title: MiniMax Music 2.6 API Playground
hero.description: Test `minimax-music-2.6` on APIDot with the same async API shape used in production. Generate full songs from prompts, add lyrics when needed, switch to instrumental mode, and control sample rate, bitrate, and export format.

overview.title: Ship MiniMax Music 2.6 API with a clean async AI music workflow
paragraphs: MiniMax Music 2.6 API on APIDot is built for teams that need reliable AI song generation without inventing a custom audio pipeline. You submit one async request, receive a task id immediately, and then query the final music result through the shared music detail endpoint.

The current request surface stays narrow on purpose: prompt, optional lyrics, Lyrics Optimize, instrumental mode, and audio output control. That makes MiniMax Music 2.6 API easier to validate in the playground and easier to ship in production code.

If you already know the style and structure you want, send prompt plus lyrics. If you only have the musical direction, enable Lyrics Optimize and let MiniMax Music 2.6 generate the lyrical structure for you. If you need pure background music, switch on instrumental mode and keep the request even simpler.

capabilities.title: What MiniMax Music 2.6 API is designed to do

items.title: AI song generation from prompt and lyrics
items.description: Use MiniMax Music 2.6 API to generate complete songs from a style prompt and optional lyrics, which is useful when your app needs fast prompt-to-song workflows instead of manual composition tooling.

items.title: Lyrics optimizer for lighter input
items.description: When lyrics are not ready, enable Lyrics Optimize so the request can still move forward from a descriptive music prompt. This is the practical path for fast creative ideation and first-pass demo generation.

items.title: Instrumental mode for BGM and utility audio
items.description: Turn on instrumental mode when you need background music, mood loops, product demos, or creator assets without vocals. Instrumental mode also removes the need to provide lyrics.

items.title: Audio output control for production delivery
items.description: Set `sample_rate`, `bitrate`, and `format` in `audio_setting` so the exported file matches your downstream player, storage policy, or delivery constraints.

useCases.title: Where MiniMax Music 2.6 API fits best
useCases.description: MiniMax Music 2.6 API works well when you need predictable async music generation, explicit pricing, and a prompt-to-audio workflow that developers can integrate quickly.
items: AI music generation for creator tools and media editors
Instrumental background music for video, apps, and demos
Lyrics-assisted song prototyping for entertainment products
Internal prototyping of music features before full-scale rollout
Async music API integration with webhook or polling orchestration

quickstart.title: Start using MiniMax Music 2.6 API in three steps

steps.title: Create your APIDot API key
steps.description: Generate an API key first, keep it on the server side, and use the same credential model you already use for APIDot image, video, and chat requests.

steps.title: Top up for fixed music pricing
steps.description: MiniMax Music 2.6 pricing on this page is fixed at 20 credits ($0.10) per generation, so you can estimate cost before you submit production traffic.

steps.title: Submit and query the final audio
steps.description: Send the async submit request to `/api/generate/submit`, store the returned `task_id`, then query `/api/generate/detail/music?task_id=...` until the job finishes.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about MiniMax Music 2.6 API

items.question: What is MiniMax Music 2.6 API best used for?
items.answer: MiniMax Music 2.6 API is best used for async AI song generation, lyrics-assisted composition, instrumental music generation, and production workflows where you want explicit audio export controls.

items.question: How much does MiniMax Music 2.6 cost on APIDot?
items.answer: Pricing is fixed at 20 credits ($0.10) per generation on this page, with no separate tier matrix exposed for this MiniMax Music 2.6 API workflow.

items.question: When do I need to send lyrics?
items.answer: You should send lyrics when you want direct lyrical control. If `lyrics_optimizer` is enabled or `is_instrumental` is true, lyrics are not required.

items.question: Can I use MiniMax Music 2.6 API for instrumental output only?
items.answer: Yes. Set `is_instrumental` to `true` and keep the request focused on style, mood, pacing, and arrangement without supplying lyrics.

items.question: How do I retrieve the final music result?
items.answer: After submit returns `task_id`, query `/api/generate/detail/music?task_id=...` to read the final `audio_url`, `audio_id`, `title`, `duration`, and prompt metadata.

items.question: Which audio controls are supported right now?
items.answer: The current MiniMax Music 2.6 API page supports `audio_setting.sample_rate`, `audio_setting.bitrate`, and `audio_setting.format` only, matching the official Poyo docs shape.

items.question: Is this page aligned to the official MiniMax Music 2.6 request format?
items.answer: Yes. The playground, API examples, and validation rules on this page follow the current official Poyo MiniMax Music 2.6 documentation rather than speculative or broader music feature sets.
